codeReview.summary: The module split and file-size constraints were achieved, but the change set is not a pure behavior-preserving refactor and the added regression tests do not fully enforce the stated parity requirements.

issues.description: The refactor changes visible policy-row key copy by switching to hardcoded `displayId` values (from `mocks.ts`) instead of preserving the pre-refactor render path (`shortHex(peer.pubkey, 6, 4)` in the prior monolith). This breaks the feature's stated "no visible behavior change" expectation.

The VAL-DSH-002 regression test explicitly allows Lock alongside Stop and only checks presence of `Stop Signer`; it does not assert CTA exclusivity. The same test file also omits exact VAL-DSH-003 row-copy/TTL assertions, so required parity checks are incomplete.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ui-builder` skill's tests-first guidance is underspecified for pure refactor/split features, which led to implementation-first churn and late regression-test tightening.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 39ca7784-c51e-4ee9-b436-4a2cf3fc953d shows large file creation/split work before DashboardRefactor.test.tsx was added and iteratively fixed; handoff.skillFeedback also suggests clarifying this procedure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Planning artifacts for this feature are ambiguous: feature text says preserve exact behavior, while milestone contract assertions emphasize specific post-refactor audit details/copy. This creates avoidable scope ambiguity for workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (`dashboard-refactor-split` expectedBehavior: identical headings/CTAs/body copy before vs after) versus validation-contract.md VAL-DSH-101 audit-detail requirements.

summary: I reviewed feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit b0a1059. Structure/organization goals were met, but there are blocking parity and test-spec gaps, so this review is marked fail.
